title: Cryo-ET data acquisition, tomogram reconstruction, analysis and segmentation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bos9b","text":"Arctis lamellas were imaged on a Krios G4 at 300 kV with Selectris X energy filter and Falcon 4i camera (Thermo Fisher Scientific), using Tomo5 software.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Acquire Atlas overviews at 125X with 5x5 tiles.
Collect search maps of each lamella site with energy filter @ 11500X magnification.

Acquire tilt series at a nominal magnification of 42,000X (2.93A pixels) with a 2° tilt increment in an angular range from −48° to 60°. Save the frames in the EER file format The tilt scheme is on purpose non-symmetric because, due to the high lamella pre-tilt of -18°, one branch is limited by the maximum stage tilt and the other one by the lamella thickness.
Motion-correct the EER frames with Relion’s implementation of Motioncorr2.
Segment the tomogram automatically using Membrain-Seg (pretrained model v9). To deal with the complex and narrow membrane architecture of MLVs, segment these with TomosegmemTV.

Open an unbinned, CTF-corrected tomogram suitable for membrane thickness measurement in IMOD. Rotate the lamella so that it is perfectly flat in both xz- and yz-projections. Crop the tomogram's z-range to a box, where the interleaflet is oriented parallel to the z-axis and clearly visible.
Filter the resulting slab in EMAN2 with a low-pass filter to the Nyquist frequency.
Coarse align and CTF-correct the tilt series in IMOD.
Align and reconstruct the final tomograms at the desired binning with the weighted backprojection algorithm in AreTomo.
Denoise the bin4 (11.72px) tomograms using cryo-CARE.
Trim the volume to the multi lamellar vesicle's spatial expansions.
Z-project the volume to 16-bit TIF-files and load them into Gwyddion.
Invert the contrast, set minimum intensity to 0 and extract profiles of similar length perpendicular to membrane stacks. Export the measurements as comma separated value files.

Average the profiles in OriginLab 2023, detect their peaks, distance to each other, and calculate full width at half maximum (FWHM). If no clear peaks can be found automatically, set the peaks manually.
Use Amira.3D to merge and refine both Membrain-Seg and TomosegmemTV segmentations.
Render the final volume in ChimeraX.
creator: Felix Kraus
